memoryArtifacts.headline: The Garrett Family in England.pdf
memoryArtifacts.description: This extensive research appears to have been completed by John E. Garrett Jr. who also provided the Garrett Family Genealogy attached in several of the same records. Everyone in this document is not yet tagged. Link - https://www.yumpu.com/en/document/read/20936828/the-garretts-in-england-garrett-family-genealogy

popularStory.headline:
Sir George Neville 1st Baron Latimer
(1414 – 30 December 1469)
popularStory.description:
Sir George Neville, 1st Baron Latimer

George Neville, 1st Baron Latimer or Latymer (c. 1407 – 30 December 1469) was an English peer.

George Neville was the fifth son of Ralph de Neville, 1st Earl of …

zh.lifeSummary: 當George Neville Lord Latimer於1415年出生在英國英格蘭達勒姆拉比 煙雨 凱福斯東時，他的父親Ralph Neville52歲，母親Joan de Beaufort40歲。他和Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1469年12月30日享年54歲，埋葬在英國越可爾維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

English: variant of   Noel  .
English: variant of   Newhall  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Latimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattimer
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .
German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When George Neville Lord Latimer was born in 1415, in Raby with Keverstone, Durham, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ralph Neville, was 52 and his mother, Joan de Beaufort, was 40. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp. He died on 30 December 1469, at the age of 54, and was buried in Well, Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: George Neville Lord Latimer(이)가 1415년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 라비 위스 케버스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph Neville(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Beaufort(은)는 40세였다. 그는 Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1469년 12월 30일 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 웰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Neville Lord Latimer nasceu em 1415, em Raby with Keverstone, Durham, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ralph Neville, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Beaufort, tinha 40 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp. Ele faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1469, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Well, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Raby-With-Keverstone, Durham: раби вит кивирстоун, дурам, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Neville Lord Latimer родился в 1415 г. в местности раби вит кивирстоун, дурам, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ralph Neville было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Beaufort, 40. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp. Он умер 30 декабря 1469 г. в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности велл, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Neville Lord Latimer nació en 1415, en Raby with Keverstone, Durham, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Ralph Neville, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Joan de Beaufort, tenía 40 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp. Murió el 30 de diciembre de 1469, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Well, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
